lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I want you to find a comfortable position. [pause] If you're standing, sit. If you're sitting, don't move.
Breathe in. [pause] Four count. [pause] Release.
Again. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
Your body knows how to survive. It's been surviving all night.
The ones outside — they are not your responsibility. [pause] Say that back to yourself. Not my responsibility.
Breathe in the knowledge that you made a choice. [pause] You are here because you chose to be here.
Not because you were afraid. Because you were honest.
In through the nose. [pause] The air is cold tonight. That's real. That's okay.
The cold keeps some of them inside too. [pause] That's not your burden to carry.
Scan your body. Start at the crown. [pause] Is there tension?
Release it. [pause] It was never yours to begin with.
Down the face. The jaw. [pause] People clench their jaws when they're listening to someone lie.
Are you listening to a lie right now?
Unclench. [pause] Breathe.
The shoulders. [pause] This is where we carry the weight of people who are no longer here.
Let them down. [pause] They've already let you go.
The chest. The heart. [pause] It's still beating. That's remarkable, really.
It's still beating in a world that stopped beating {{ELAPSED}} ago.
Keep it beating. That's the only work you have left.
Abdomen. [pause] Where we hold the things we won't say.
There are words in your stomach right now. [pause] They don't need to come out.
Let them stay. Some truths are meant to be buried.
The legs. [pause] Are they restless?
They want to run. To hide. To find somewhere deeper.
Remind them they're already safe. [pause] This room is safe because you decided it was.
The feet. [pause] The foundation.
Feel the floor beneath you. [pause] It's solid. It won't betray you.
Unlike most things.
Now I want you to think about forgiveness. [pause] Not forgiving others.
Forgiving yourself for surviving.
You're alive. [pause] Someone else is not.
That's not your fault. [pause] Say it. It's not my fault.
Again. Louder if you can. It's not my fault.
The Order teaches that silence is a kind of prayer. [pause] That listening is a kind of grace.
Have you been listening all night? [pause] To the sounds outside. To the quiet between them.
That listening has changed you. [pause] You're different than you were at seven.
You're more. [pause] Or you're less. [pause] I'm not sure which is which anymore.
Breathe. [pause] Ka-vah-tela-ka. [pause] The breath is light moving through dark.
Picture a door. [pause] Your door. [pause] Your threshold.
On the other side is morning. [pause] Three hours and fifty-six minutes until morning.
Three hours and fifty-five minutes now.
Can you feel time moving? [pause] It's faster when you're not living it.
When you're observing it from somewhere else.
I want you to know that you don't have to open that door when it comes. [pause] You can keep it closed.
The world will keep spinning. [pause] The ones outside will have consequences.
The ones inside — the ones listening — we are the clean ones.
We chose the narrow path. [pause] The hard path.
That's enough. [pause] That's more than enough.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] And release.
You're safe. [pause] You'll be safe tomorrow. [pause] And the day after that.
Stay with me until the light comes.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 153 kills. 8-hour mark. Up 14% from 2168 same hour.
East Side reporting sustained activity. Capitol Square perimeter holding. State Street remains hottest corridor.
"The ones inside are the clean ones. We chose the narrow path."
Temperature at {{CLOCK}}: 26°F. Fog over Lake Mendota at 1.5 mi visibility. NW winds 16 mph gusts to 24.
